summary: Latin America presents a low-risk environment for wind energy operations. Political stability indices are at multi-year highs, regulatory frameworks are supportive, and cyber threat actors have shown minimal interest in the regional energy sector.

lead_indicators.text: Brazil wind energy sector shows stable political and regulatory environment following recent elections, continued government support for wind expansion
LATAM regional trade agreements reducing tariff barriers for wind energy components, improving supply chain economics
Wind turbine supply chain resilience improving in Latin America despite rare earth pressures from Chinese export controls
LATAM cyber threat landscape remains low intensity for industrial operators, no active campaigns targeting wind energy infrastructure
No active OT threat campaigns targeting LATAM wind energy sector confirmed by threat intelligence providers
